114: In 2001, two years after Yoshikage Kira's defeat, Koichi Hirose arrives in Naples, Italy at the request of Jotaro Kujo to obtain a skin sample from a teenager named Haruno Shiobana. Koichi ends up being scammed by Haruno, who now goes by the name of Giorno Giovanna. While escaping from Koichi, Giorno uses his Stand Gold Experience to transform Koichi's luggage into a frog. Giorno then has an altercation with a gangster named Leaky Eye Luca, which results in Luca taking damage and being knocked out. Later, Giorno encounters Bruno Bucciarati, who suspects him of harming Luca, and the two battle as Giorno reveals his goal to join the organization.
115: A flashback shows Giorno's troubled childhood and how he was inspired by a gangster. In the present, Giorno faces Bucciarati's Stand, Sticky Fingers. After a fierce battle, Giorno decides to spare Bucciarati and asks to join the Passione organization to take down its boss and improve Naples.
116: Bucciarati introduces Giorno to the capo Polpo. To join Passione, Giorno must keep a cigarette lighter burning for 24 hours. While doing so, he is attacked by Polpo's remote Stand, Black Sabbath, after a janitor accidentally reignites the lighter.
117: Giorno and Koichi team up to defeat Black Sabbath by exposing it to sunlight. Giorno is admitted into Passione but kills Polpo by turning a gun into a banana. Later, Bucciarati takes Giorno to meet the rest of the team: Abbacchio, Narancia, Mista, and Fugo.
118: The team learns of Polpo's death and departs for Capri to retrieve his hidden fortune. On the yacht, members of the team begin to vanish mysteriously. Abbacchio uses his Stand, Moody Blues, to investigate the situation.
119: Abbacchio's past as a police officer is revealed. Using Moody Blues, the team discovers the enemy Stand, Soft Machine, which has deflated the yacht. Bucciarati defeats the user, Mario Zucchero, and rescues his teammates.
120: The team tortures Zucchero and learns he has a partner waiting on Capri. Giorno and Mista head to the island first. Mista uses his Stand, Sex Pistols, to locate the partner, Sale, and pursues him onto a truck.
121: Mista's past and the luck surrounding his Stand are revealed. On Capri, Mista battles Sale and his Stand, Kraft Work, which can fix objects in place. Mista uses his Sex Pistols to outsmart and defeat Sale.
122: Bucciarati is promoted to capo and given the mission to protect Trish Una, the boss's daughter. Meanwhile, Narancia is ambushed by Formaggio of the Hitman Team, who uses his Stand, Little Feet, to shrink him.
123: Narancia continues to shrink while desperately trying to call the team. He uses his Stand, Aerosmith, to track Formaggio through the city's sewers. A flashback shows the Hitman Team's discovery of their members' brutal executions by the boss.
124: Narancia's tragic backstory is revealed. He manages to defeat Formaggio by setting the area on fire with Aerosmith's bullets. The team then receives orders to travel to Pompeii to retrieve a key for a high-speed vehicle.
125: Giorno, Fugo, and Abbacchio arrive in Pompeii. Fugo is dragged into a mirror world by Illuso, while his Stand, Purple Haze, appears in the real world. Fugo's past and his Stand's deadly virus are explained.
126: Abbacchio and Giorno enter the battle against Illuso. Abbacchio is partially trapped in the mirror world but manages to pass the key to Giorno. Giorno infects himself with Purple Haze's virus to defeat Illuso, then uses his Stand to create a cure.
127: The team heads to the Naples railway station and finds a turtle, Coco Jumbo, which has a Stand that provides a safe room. They are attacked by Prosciutto and Pesci; Prosciutto's Stand, The Grateful Dead, begins aging everyone on the train.
128: Mista is nearly killed by Pesci's Stand, Beach Boy. Bucciarati emerges to fight Prosciutto, whose aging ability is weakening the entire team. Bucciarati drags Prosciutto off the moving train to stop the aging process.
129: Pesci becomes a deadly threat as he overcomes his cowardice to avenge Prosciutto. Bucciarati manages to defeat Pesci in a brutal encounter. The team then faces Melone, who creates a Stand-child, Baby Face, to track and eliminate them.
130: Baby Face Jr. attacks Giorno on the way to Venice. Giorno uses the battle to evolve his Stand's ability, allowing him to replace lost body parts. He eventually turns the tide against the creature.
131: Giorno defeats Baby Face Jr. and sends a poisonous snake to kill Melone. The team receives the boss's final orders through a recording. They are then attacked by Ghiaccio, whose Stand, White Album, freezes the entire environment.
132: Mista and Giorno battle Ghiaccio near Venice. Mista risks his life by using his own blood to block Ghiaccio's vision, allowing Giorno to deliver the final blow. Giorno then heals Mista's extensive injuries.
133: The team arrives in Venice. Bucciarati takes Trish to the top of a bell tower to meet the boss, but discovers the boss intends to kill her. Bucciarati rebels and faces the boss's Stand, King Crimson, which can erase time.
134: Bucciarati survives a fatal blow thanks to Giorno's intervention but is now in a semi-deceased state. The boss retreats to maintain his anonymity. Most of the team decides to join Bucciarati's rebellion, except for Fugo.
135: The team stops for lunch and is attacked by Squalo and Tiziano of the boss's elite guard. Squalo's Stand, Clash, can travel between liquids, while Tiziano's Talking Head forces Narancia to lie.
136: Narancia battles Squalo and Tiziano alone while his teammates are incapacitated. He manages to track down the users and defeats them both with Aerosmith, allowing the group to continue toward the airport.
137: The group hijacks a plane but is attacked by Notorious B.I.G., an indestructible Stand that activates after its user's death. Giorno is forced to sacrifice his arms to get the Stand off the plane.
138: Trish awakens her Stand, Spice Girl, which can soften any surface. She defeats Notorious B.I.G. by making it target itself. The group reaches Sardinia, where the boss's past is hidden.
139: The boss's early life in Sardinia and his split personality, Doppio, are revealed. Doppio encounters Risotto Nero, the leader of the Hitman Team. The boss gives Doppio the ability to see into the future with Epitaph.
140: Doppio and Risotto engage in a deadly game of cat and mouse. Risotto uses Metallica to manipulate iron in Doppio's body. However, the boss's planning leads to Risotto being fatally shot by Narancia's Aerosmith.
141: The boss executes Risotto and manages to kill Abbacchio while he is investigating. Before dying, Abbacchio manages to leave a death mask behind to reveal the boss's face to the rest of the team.
142: The team searches for the boss's identity using the mask. They are contacted by a mysterious person from Rome who knows the boss's secret. They are then ambushed by Cioccolata and Secco, who use deadly mold and ground-liquifying Stands.
143: The team struggles to climb higher to avoid Cioccolata's mold. Giorno and Mista pursue Cioccolata in a helicopter, while Bucciarati faces off against Secco. Bucciarati discovers that his body is slowly deteriorating.
144: Giorno defeats Cioccolata in a famously long beat-down after outsmarting his surgical Stand abilities. Secco learns of his partner's defeat and realizes the mission is in jeopardy.
145: Bucciarati defeats Secco by using sound to disrupt his hearing. However, Bucciarati's body begins to fail completely. He encounters Doppio near the Colosseum, who he mistakes for a friendly ally.
146: The boss arrives at the Colosseum and encounters Jean Pierre Polnareff, the man from Rome. Polnareff reveals he has a Stand arrow. In a desperate move, he uses it on his own Stand to protect the arrow from the boss.
147: Polnareff's Stand evolves into Chariot Requiem, which puts everyone in Rome to sleep and swaps their souls. The team wakes up in different bodies and must work together to retrieve the arrow from the uncontrollable Requiem.
148: The group attempts to retrieve the arrow but is hindered by Chariot Requiem's protective powers. The boss kills Narancia while hiding in another person's body. The group realizes the boss is amongst them.
149: Doppio dies while inhabiting Bucciarati's old body. The team discovers the secret to Chariot Requiem's power. The boss manages to dissipate the Stand and nearly obtains the arrow, but Trish intervenes.
150: Bucciarati's soul finally departs as everyone returns to their original bodies. Giorno manages to obtain the arrow and evolves his Stand into Gold Experience Requiem, which has the power to nullify any action back to zero.
151: Gold Experience Requiem traps the boss, Diavolo, in an infinite loop of death. A flashback shows Mista's encounter with the Stand Rolling Stones, which predicted the fates of Bucciarati, Narancia, and Abbacchio.
152: The team's journey concludes as Giorno is instated as the new leader of Passione. Polnareff's soul remains in the turtle to advise him. The survivors look toward a new future for the organization.
